title: Apple Just Built WSL for the Mac (Container Machines)
summary: Apple Container Machines is a new feature providing lightweight, persistent Linux environments on Mac, built on Apple's Container project and optimized for Apple Silicon. It functions similarly to WSL for Windows, enabling developers to test on Linux while developing on macOS with shared home directory access.
writeup: The video covers Apple Container Machines, a feature that provides lightweight, persistent Linux environments on Mac, built on Apple's Container project and optimized for Apple Silicon. It draws a direct comparison to WSL on Windows: developers work in macOS while testing on Linux, with shared home directory access between the two. The takeaway is that Linux workflows can run alongside native macOS development without a separate machine or heavier virtualization setup.
